wifi_idle_24:

Single I/Q snapshot collected at FOB Spectrum Manager, Marine Installation perimeter. Metadata reflects center frequency 2.437 GHz, bw_MHz 20.0, sample rate 30.0 MSPS, gain 30 dB, hardware USRP-X300/UBX160/VERT2450, noise floor -96 dBm, and factory calibration.

(U) Top Emitters Identified  
Primary emitter assessed as a Wi‑Fi 2.4 GHz access point using OFDM, with protocol inferred as WiFi-2.4 and device class hypothesis “wifi-AP.” Activity level is low/idle: estimated burst count 10, duty cycle estimate 0.25%, and signal strength band assessed as medium, consistent with periodic AP beacon transmissions.

(U) Anomalies / Unauthorized Activity  
No significant anomalies identified in the provided capture. Classifier anomaly_flag is “nominal,” and the observed emission pattern aligns with expected idle AP beacon behavior rather than jamming, spoofing, or unauthorized high-duty-cycle occupancy.

(U) Recommended Spectrum Manager Actions  
Maintain current monitoring posture for the 2.437 GHz / 20.0 MHz segment and correlate with known/base-authorized Wi‑Fi infrastructure on the installation perimeter. No immediate mitigation is indicated; continue periodic captures to confirm persistence of nominal beacon-only behavior and detect any future deviation in duty cycle, emitter count, or protocol signature.

(U) Confidence  
HIGH — Assessment is supported by coherent metadata and classifier output with confidence 0.88, and all observed features are internally consistent with nominal idle Wi‑Fi AP activity.
